title: test/e2e: use custom network config dir where needed
body:
Since commit f250560a8043 the play kube command uses its own network. this is racy be disgn because we create the network followed by creating/running pod/containers. This means in the meantime another prune or reset process could wipe out the network config because we have to share the network config directory by desgin in the test.

The problem is we only have one host netns which is shared between tests. If the network config dir is not shared we cannot make conflict checks for interface names and ip address. This results in different tests trying to use the same interface and/or ip address which will cause runtime failures in CNI and netavark.

The only solution I see is to make sure only the reset/prune tests are using a custom network dir. This makes sure they do not wipe configs that are otherwise required by other parallel running tests.

Fixes #17946

@edsantiago PTAL, unfortunately this means any new prune/reset test which forgets this call will cause new flakes but I see no way around that other than human review.
LGTM at first glance. I've cherrypicked it onto #17831 and we'll see how it goes.
Downloaded and examined all CI logs from this PR; no new flakes, just the "no logs from conmon" one.
